title: compress_image uses deprecated tempfile.mktemp and callers never clean up temp files
description: compress_image() in social/posting.py uses tempfile.mktemp() (lines 138, 154) which is deprecated because it creates a race condition between name generation and file creation. More critically, the caller _upload_blob() calls compress_image() but never cleans up the returned temp file, causing a disk leak. The docstring on compress_image explicitly warns 'the caller is responsible for cleanup' but neither BlueskyClient._upload_blob() nor any other caller performs cleanup. Each image post that requires compression leaks a temp JPEG file. Over days/weeks of automated posting this accumulates.
requirements: Replace tempfile.mktemp() with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(delete=False) or use a context manager pattern
Add cleanup in _upload_blob() after the upload completes (success or failure)
Use try/finally to ensure cleanup even on upload exceptions
Consider returning a context manager from compress_image that auto-cleans on exit
done_criteria: No calls to deprecated tempfile.mktemp() remain in the codebase
Compressed temp files are cleaned up after upload in _upload_blob()
Cleanup occurs even if the upload raises an exception
No temp file leak after running the social posting pipeline
context: tempfile.mktemp is deprecated per Python docs: 'Use mkstemp() instead.' The race condition means another process could create a file with the same name between mktemp() returning and write_bytes() creating it. The disk leak is confirmed: _upload_blob() at line 487 calls compress_image() but has no cleanup code.
